new_candidates.description: RESOLVED-APPLIED to canon 2026-05-29 (Jordan-directed adopt/tune). Deterministic+stochastic resolver ratified as the canonical Domain Action resolution method in params/factions/stats_1_7_scale.md: P_success=clamp(0.50+0.10*M, 0.05, 0.90) with graded degree bands; constant 0.10/pt leverage (closes the sigma-leverage non-uniformity by construction); bare-stat-vs-Ob superseded for faction Domain Actions. Bare-stat checks migrated (Suppress Failure->Stab-1, Rebuttal Overwhelming->+1 hooks preserved; §1.4 co-designed with §1.3 recovery). Dice systems (combat/social/mass-battle) unchanged. Precedent-backed (COIN/structural-contest, matrix entry 2). Faction sigma-leverage is NON-UNIFORM across the faction stat range (armature A6, computed MC). Bare-stat pool => baseline = stat, so sigma-leverage ~ 1/sqrt(stat): 0.354/pt at stat 2 (+17pct vs 0.30 ref) decaying to 0.189/pt at stat 7 (-37pct, OUT OF BAND low). A point of stat buys decaying leverage as the faction strengthens, compounding the post-F2-ruling punching-up wall (strong rivals hard to act on AND strong-faction stat investment leverage-poor). Distinct from ED-868 (collapse loops) and the artifact-error ED-865/866; this is the underlying attribute-economy property. Aggregating the faction pool (cf. mass-battle ER-9) flattens it. STAGE-4 TESTED: sigma-leverage non-uniformity is NOT fixable by pool aggregation - sigma-leverage ~ 1/sqrt(baseline) is irreducibly non-uniform for any dice pool (flat-agg narrows spread 0.165->0.053 but pushes leverage below band; x2 mult makes low-stat too high, spread 0.233). Only a deterministic+stochastic resolver (constant leverage = slope by construction) fixes it. Strengthens the deterministic+stochastic option on the sigma-leverage dimension specifically. RESOLVER SPEC PRODUCED (domain_action_resolver_spec): tuned deterministic+stochastic candidate P=clamp(0.50+0.10*M, 0.05, 0.90) with graded degree bands; constant 0.10/pt leverage (ED-874 fixed by construction), floor degeneracy 0.069->0.300, punching-up 0.010->0.050, capped ceiling; all Stage-4 checks PASS; Domain Echo OUTPUT unchanged (drop-in). Interface note covers Suppress, Parliamentary Rebuttal, §1.4 Accounting Stability Check (co-design w/ §1.3 recovery, CC-4). Candidate ready for Jordan adopt/tune; NOT applied to canon. Params PROPOSED/untuned. PRECEDENT-BACKED (ners_historical_precedent_matrix entry 2, COIN/structural-contest literature): real political contests are structure-decisive with a stochastic tail, NOT low-variance coin-flips — exactly what deterministic-odds+stochastic-resolution delivers. SECOND justification: the d+s resolver is also what makes the ED-868 faction-collapse bound (Reconstitution) reachable (bare dice leave it near-unreachable at low Influence).

Mass-battle Command sigma-leverage is HIGH at low Command (armature A6, computed): 0.500/pt at Cmd 2 decaying to in-band at Cmd 4-7. Low-Command early-game generals get oversized per-point leverage. Closes attribute_weight_standard GAP #2 (was magnitude-unverified). Same 1/sqrt(baseline) mechanism as ED-874; non-uniform across range. NOTE 2026-05-29: NOT addressed by the d+s resolver (mass battle stays dice per scope boundary — healthy aggregated pool). The low-Command sigma-leverage is an edge property of an otherwise NERS-PASS dice system; left as a P3 flag (no clean dice-side fix without disrupting the aggregated pool). Owner decision whether to act.

RESOLVED-APPLIED to canon 2026-05-28 (commit 2327adc4). Trigger-5 firing is NON-MONOTONIC in faction strength because Condition C stacks a pool-size cliff (pool >= 6) onto a magnitude threshold (net <= -2). Computed firing rate by defender pool (recovery-independent MC): Cmd-1 (2D)=0pct [Cond A blocks], Cmd-2 (4D)=1.9pct [needs rare net<=-2], Cmd-3 (6D)=16.6pct [pool>=6 auto-fires C on ANY failure], declining to ~8pct for strong factions. A single Command point (Cmd 2->3) jumps battle->Stability exposure 8.7x. Mid-tier factions are MOST exposed to battle-driven Stability loss; the weakest are shielded. Consider smoothing C (e.g. severity scaled by margin rather than a hard pool>=6 auto-fire). Corrects the earlier hand-traced assumption that weak-faction battles drive a deterministic Stability spiral (CC-2). STRENGTHENED (CC-5): with the canonical Institutional Consolidation recovery, the cliff propagates to COLLAPSE — compound collapse jumps ~25x from Cmd 2 (0.9pct) to Cmd 3 (22.6pct), declining at Cmd 4 (18.6pct), because a fired Trigger-5 denies the deterministic clean-season +1 recovery. A single Command point swinging collapse 25x is a robustness defect -> P1. STAGE-4 TESTED (sim_faction_remediation): Fix-A = remove the pool>=6 clause from Condition C's GATE (keep it in the COST table). Firing curve goes monotonic/flat (~1-2pct, Cmd-3 spike removed) and 100pct of genuine routs (net<=-2) are still punished; only disciplined net 0/-1 large-force withdrawals are spared (matches canon intent). Recommend apply. Independent of the ED-865/874 resolver fork.
new_candidates.jordan_decision: Fix-A applied per Jordan instruction; faction_layer_v30 §0 + Trigger-5 patched; canonical_sources SHA refreshed

new_candidates.description: RESOLVED (was UNREAD, not absent). The faction Stability-recovery mechanic EXISTS: faction_layer_v30 §1.3 Institutional Consolidation = DETERMINISTIC +1 Stability on any Trigger-1-5-free ('clean') season; canon explicitly states it 'replaces the abstract simulator recovery rule.' Conditional paths: peace treaty, recapture territory, Parliamentary Overwhelming, Church Absolution, Lowenritter endorsement; seasonal cap +-2. §1.4 Accounting Stability Check is a cascade-DAMAGE check (fires only on >=2 attr-pts lost), not recovery. Compound sim re-run with this canonical mechanic; SANITY passes; collapse magnitudes now trustworthy. Do NOT file as an open gap.

RE-ASSESSED — NOT a contradiction (corrects my own ED-878 framing). Full read of threadwork_v30 §3.2: L622 base table ('FR Lock/Dissolution any scale: -1 additional; min total -2') and L631-633 PP-196 ('Object/Personal: -1 total [scale 0 + FR -1]; Relational: -2 total [-1 scale + -1 FR]; FR surcharge cap-EXEMPT') describe ONE coherent rule across scales, not two competing values. The 'min total -2' holds at Relational+ where FR is typically used; PP-196 clarifies the smaller-scale totals and the cap-exemption. Independently corroborated at L177 (fieldwork note cites 'FR/Binding cap-exempt per PP-196' as established). PP-196 is a consistent elaboration, not a divergence. No patch needed; do not file as a contradiction. (CC-4 lesson: read fully before asserting a defect.)
new_candidates.jordan_decision: n/a — false contradiction, resolved by full read

new_candidates.description: Combat secondary consistency items (consolidated, C3/C5/C6). C3: out-of-bounds x Pool-Floor-5 interaction underspecified (does the floor apply when a combatant is pushed OOB?). C5: Health super-linear at low Endurance via (End+6)(MW+1) MW step (now also captured quantitatively in ED-826 + the I-1 win-rate refinement below). C6: Strength damage-multiplier vs Power terminology ambiguity. Combat is mechanically sound (NERS PASS); these are clarity/consistency, not balance. RE-ASSESSED: not evidence-patchable. C6 'Strength multiplier' appears only in Garrison Strength (settlement-defense formula, unrelated to the personal-combat Strength stat); personal combat uses clear floor(STR/2) terms — no live terminology conflict found. C3 out-of-bounds x Pool-Floor: no defined OOB mechanic surfaces in combat_v30 — it is underspecified-by-ABSENCE, and specifying what OOB should do is owner-intent (design), not an evidence patch. C5 already captured in ED-826. Combat is NERS-PASS; these are low-priority clarity items requiring Jordan's design intent, retained as such.
new_candidates.jordan_decision: pending — C3/C6 need owner design intent (clarity, low priority); C5 -> ED-826

new_candidates.description: RESOLVED-APPLIED 2026-05-29. faction_layer §Varfell and params/bg/core asserted 'Varfell expansion is purely military / does not convert populations ideologically' — the rationale for the 2026-04-19 Cultural REFORMATION strike (VTM-dependent action; VTM was a placeholder, validly struck). But the Conviction-Track redesign (conviction_track_v30) reintroduced the ideological-conversion capability VTM-FREE as Cultural RECLAMATION (Varfell: Influence vs Ob 2, target-territory Piety -1 toward Einhir Restoration pole). The stale 'purely military' prose was never reconciled. Per Jordan 2026-05-29: Varfell takes ALL universal faction actions and has Cultural Reclamation as a non-military Piety-ALIGNMENT vector (NOT a control transfer, NOT a victory path). Reconciled the prose in faction_layer_v30 §Varfell + params/bg/core (L5 comment + L226 row) toward the live mechanic; Reformation + VTM remain struck. SOLE victory condition remains Peninsular Sovereignty (GD-1) — no faction-specific/religious/non-military victory path. Documentation reconciliation; no mechanical change (Cultural Reclamation was already live).
affected_docs: canon/supersession_register.yaml (CR-STRIKE/VTM entries still carry the historical 'purely military' rationale — flagged for editorial owner to add a forward-pointer to ED-880; not edited here to preserve the audit trail)
new_candidates.source: Jordan flag 2026-05-29; repo investigation (conviction_track_v30 Cultural Reclamation)
new_candidates.jordan_decision: reconcile toward live Cultural Reclamation; Reformation/VTM stay struck; Piety-alignment only (no control, no victory); peninsula sole victory (GD-1)

new_candidates.description: RESOLVED-APPLIED to canon 2026-05-29 (Jordan-directed ratification). GD-3 (Revolt -> Insurgency -> Faction) has NO down-path: the pipeline can escalate but cannot DISSOLVE. Per ners_historical_precedent_matrix entry 4 (RAND, How Insurgencies End, 89 cases): insurgent defeat is the MODAL outcome; loss of sanctuary/sponsorship is the strongest predictor of defeat; ~10yr average duration. GD-3 currently cannot represent the most common real outcome -> NERS R-FAIL on the missing down-path. Recommend owner-authored multi-path dissolution: military defeat / sponsor-withdrawal / amnesty-negotiated / else-persist, with sponsor-withdrawal the high-value add. Surfaced by the precedent reconciliation, not by this session's faction-collapse work (collapse itself is bounded — ED-868). RESOLVED-CANDIDATE (insurgency_dissolution_proposal): tested 4-path down-path (military defeat / sponsor withdrawal / amnesty / persist) subsuming pipeline Options A/B as the military exit + adding the RAND-dominant sponsor path. 8k MC: defeat MODAL (0.97), sponsor-loss the dominant defeat driver tracking P(sponsor-loss) per RAND, amnesty a minority exit, stalemate reachable; Stage-4 found+fixed 2 defects (sponsor misattribution, amnesty over-fire). Completes pipeline forward-flags INSURGENCY-DISSOLUTION-001 + INSURGENCY-PROMOTED-DISSOLUTION-001. NOT written to canon (GD-3 §B requires Jordan ratification). Amnesty path uses the d+s resolver (ED-865/874 cross-dep).

RESOLVED-APPLIED to canon 2026-05-29 (Jordan-directed ratification). MS engine band edges were SYMMETRIC single thresholds (20/40/60), the one behavior real regime-shifts do not show. Ratified ED-882 to ms_trajectory_v1 §5.1: (a) HYSTERESIS — recovery (rising) edges sit +8 MS above the collapse (falling) edges (28/48/68); falling direction unchanged (no regression to §5 territory crossings); path-dependent, bounded below min band width (no skip), not a trap; (b) LEADING WARNING SIGNAL within 12 MS of an edge from above (critical-slowing analog, extends Fragile spontaneous Shifting Objects into a legible pre-tip trend). Precedent: Scheffer/Holling regime-shift (matrix entry 1) — MATCH. P-07 compliant (rendered-world band effects, not substrate healing); GD-1 unaffected. Params PROPOSED/tunable (gap +8, window 12).

refinements_to_existing.note: MW-cap win-RATE/build-economy framing (I-1, combat sim): +1 Agility ~ +1 Endurance at low builds (73pct vs 71.5pct) but at high builds +1 Agi=+23pp vs +1 End=+4pp because MaxWounds caps at End 4; past the cap a min-maxer caps Endurance at 4 and dumps into Agility, distorting the attribute economy toward offense. Gives ED-826's Health-curve finding a concrete win-rate cost; argues for the continuous-Health option (no MW step). Confidence: direction high, magnitude sim-specific (confirm vs full combat sim).
continuity correction also pre-existing as ED-515; ED-873 (mine) and ED-515 cover the same fix (Ob-0.5) — flag for the consolidation owner to merge/cross-reference rather than carry two.

deferred_pending_verification: SC3 genre-weight 0.5 near-inert (D-01), SC4 Regroup-on-Spent (D-04), SC5 Focus-1 Regroup trap (D-05): NOT filed — status needs a currency check first (these are pre-flagged 2026-04-08 stress-test items whose post-patch status is unverified). File only after verifying against current social_contest canon.
T7 COMPOSURE-DRIFT: already in live ledger (do not re-file).
_cc4_update: ED-877 resolved-was-unread (recovery = §1.3 Institutional Consolidation); ED-876 strengthened P2->P1 (CC-5 collapse-level cliff). See cc4_resolution.md.
_stage4_update: Faction remediation Stage-4 tested (faction_remediation_development.md): ED-876 Fix-A is a clean win (recommend); ED-865 floor degeneracy fixable by EITHER aggregation or deterministic+stochastic; ED-874 sigma-leverage fixable ONLY by deterministic+stochastic. Design fork surfaced for Jordan.
_ed876_applied: ED-876 Fix-A applied to faction_layer_v30 (commit 2327adc4): Condition-C pool>=6 cliff removed from gate, kept as cost escalator. Ledger ingestion of resolved status is the JSONL migration's.
_ed880: Varfell 'purely military' prose reconciled to live Cultural Reclamation (Piety-alignment, non-military, VTM-free); applied to faction_layer + params/bg/core; sole victory = peninsula (GD-1).
_resolver_spec: Domain Action deterministic+stochastic resolver spec produced (tested candidate, Stage-4 PASS). Two-phase migration recommended: Domain Actions first; Suppress/Rebuttal/§1.4 as a consistency set. Not applied — Jordan's adopt/tune decision.
_precedent_reconciliation: Reconciled vs ners_historical_precedent_matrix.md (2026-05-28). NO conflicts; every faction remediation confirmed or strengthened by precedent. ED-868 collapse loop = RESOLVED-BOUNDED (matrix/Tainter: collapse=simplification+reconstitution; diagnostic Lesson-5 RETRACTED) — sim confirms bounds REACHABLE but resolver-dependent (bare dice make reconstitution near-unreachable at low Influence P~0.004; d+s makes it real -> SECOND justification for ED-865/874). ED-876 backed by morale-cascade precedent (du Picq/Clausewitz, matrix entry 7). ED-865/874 backed by COIN/structural-contest precedent (matrix entry 2: outcomes are structure-driven w/ stochastic tail = exactly deterministic-odds+stochastic-resolution). See reconciliation_session_vs_precedent_matrix.md + sim_collapse_recovery_results.md.
_gap_sweep_2026-05-29: Open-gap sweep: ED-878 RE-ASSESSED as a false contradiction (PP-196 is a consistent cross-scale elaboration, not a competing value) — corrects my own earlier framing. ED-879 C3/C6 re-assessed as needing owner design intent (not evidence-patchable); C5 -> ED-826. ED-874/875 sigma-leverage remediation = the d+s resolver candidate (Jordan adopt). ED-881 down-path = tested candidate (Jordan ratify). Net: no remaining gap is unilaterally patchable by Claude without either (a) owner design intent or (b) Jordan ratification of a tested candidate. All evidence-resolvable items resolved.
_resolver_ratified: d+s resolver RATIFIED to canon (stats_1_7_scale, commit pending). ED-874 resolved-applied; ED-865 closed with it.
_ed881_ratified: ED-881 dissolution down-path RATIFIED to insurgency_pipeline §6.2/§6.3 (4-path, RAND-validated). Closes GD-3 R-FAIL.

_ci_political_diagnostic_2026-05-30.task: resolution-and-balance diagnostic of ci_political_v30 (Stages 1-4) — NEW system, never previously decomposed
_ci_political_diagnostic_2026-05-30.self_authored_bias_note: prior handoff over-claimed 'faction cluster diagnostic COMPLETE'; actual prior scope = action layer + spillover. ci_political (+~10 provincial systems) never run.
_ci_political_diagnostic_2026-05-30.RETRACTION: In-chat draft Finding A claimed CI bonus dice cause sqrt(N) non-uniformity on Parliament ROLLS. RETRACTED: faction_layer §5.3 resolves Parliament as a DETERMINISTIC Mandate TALLY (votes=Mandate, Majority/Supermajority), not a dice pool. Error = pattern-match off ci_political §7.2 stale '10D vs Ob 2' framing instead of authoritative §5.3.

decomposition.treaty_positioning_ratification: raw d10 pool vs Ob 2 (faction_layer §3.3) — dice, NOT resolver-migrated

verdict.N: PASS — CI one-quantity, milestone ladder, card economy necessary; do NOT split CI
verdict.R: FAIL — (i) DEFINITIONAL GAP: §3.3/§3.4 opponent -floor(CI/30) undefined below 0 (CI>=90 Mandate-2 -> -1 votes -> 112.5% FOR-share). (ii) §2.2 stale 'Theocracy Unification VICTORY' vs GD-1 + §7.1.
verdict.S: FAIL — CI modifier single 'bonus dice'/'pool' paradigm (§3.2/§3.4) attaches to THREE resolution methods (tally/resolver/raw dice); inconsistent. §4.2/§4.3 Govern/Trade prose legacy-dice post-migration; §5.3 lacks CI-vote-modifier cross-ref.

findings.summary: CI political-forum modifier paradigm-mismatched to 3 resolution methods + undefined negative-vote floor
findings.fix: Parliament(tally): CI=VOTE-COUNT modifier, Church +floor(CI/20), opponent-of-Church -floor(CI/30) FLOORED AT 0. Rebuttal/Diplomacy/Domain Actions(resolver): CI=MARGIN term in M (constant +10%/pt; FLOOR 0.05 -> no wall). Treaty(raw dice): OPEN — migrate to resolver (CI=margin) OR keep dice (residual sqrt(N)).
findings.stage4: floor removes undefined-negative, monotone, no new cliff; resolver-margin uniform+floor; no new variable; fewer paradigms. High-CI Church dominance PRESERVED.
findings.open_design_choice_JORDAN: Treaty-roll migration timing: now vs defer with Suppress/Rebuttal/§1.4 batch.

findings.summary: §2.2 stale Theocracy Unification VICTORY vs GD-1 + §7.1
findings.fix: strike/reconcile §2.2 victory language to Peninsular-Sovereignty-only; Mass Seizure = recoverable territorial event, not victory trigger. Parallel ED-880. GD-1 strike-propagation does not currently list §2.2 — propagates it.
findings.stage4: prose-only; demotes Mass Seizure Phase-2 stakes.

findings.summary: §4.2/§4.3 Govern/Trade legacy-dice prose post-resolver; §5.3 lacks CI-vote-modifier cross-ref
findings.fix: annotate §4.2/§4.3 resolver-governed (point to stats_1_7_scale §Domain Action Resolution); add §5.3 cross-ref to ci_political §3.4.
findings.stage4: mechanically covered by ED-865/874; prose/co-ref only.

_ci_political_diagnostic_2026-05-30.provisional_id_note: NO live ED IDs claimed (94-ID-conflict backlog + migration-owner authority). Owner assigns final IDs on ingest.
_ci_political_diagnostic_2026-05-30.canon_writes_status: HELD for Jordan: ci_political canon edits drafted/ready (§2.2 strike, §3.2/§3.4 rewrite, §4.2/§4.3 annotation, §5.3 cross-ref). Canon write = ratification step (ED-865/874 precedent). CIP-1 has one design choice (Treaty migration timing).

_peninsular_strain_diagnostic_2026-05-30.task: resolution-and-balance diagnostic of peninsular_strain_v30 (Stages 1-4) — NEW system, never previously decomposed
_peninsular_strain_diagnostic_2026-05-30.decomposition: Strain 0-10 (accumulator, 4 spaced thresholds) + Accord 0-3 (per-territory, derived floor(mean Order)) + IP (accumulator, stepwise) + dice sub-actions (Govern/Seizure/DynProc = resolver-governed; Popular Uprising = bare-stat Military vs Ob2).

verdict.N: PASS — Strain/Accord/IP/toolkits all necessary; no redundancy; no over-apparatus.
verdict.R: PASS — Strain advance capped (+3/season) and damped (decay -1 + treaty pairs -2); §4.4 Strain<->IP cross-feed explicitly broken; Accord-0/Order-0 co-trigger deterministically ordered (§2.4b ED-632, paired safeguard); Popular Uprising binary is RECOVERABLE (Uncontrolled, not faction-death) and not a pivotal-irreversible roll (Lesson-3 scope). MS=0 Post-Calamity is intended (P-07) + safeguarded (recovery path).
verdict.S: FAIL (single, minor) — PST-1: the MS=0 Post-Calamity transition (§6.4) had NO leading warning; ED-882's ratified warning band covers only the 20/40/60 edges. By ED-882's own Scheffer/Holling precedent (deepest bifurcation = where critical-slowing warning matters most) the deepest tip should also signal. Smooth/legibility drift, not an R/loop defect.
verdict.E: PASS — accumulators are legible; thresholds intuitable; fix adds no new threshold.

finding.fix: Extend ED-882's 12-MS leading-warning window to the MS<=12 approach toward MS=0 (falling direction only). Forward-pointer added in peninsular_strain §6.4. No new threshold; MS=0 trigger + recovery unchanged.
finding.stage4: falling-direction signal only; adds no threshold, no loop, no cliff; consistent-by-construction with §5.1; NERS N/R/E unaffected, S restored.
finding.precedent: Scheffer/Holling regime-shift critical-slowing (same anchor as ED-882, matrix entry 1).

_parliamentary_transfer_diagnostic_2026-.task: resolution-and-balance diagnostic of parliamentary_transfer_v30 (Stages 1-4) — NEW system, never previously decomposed
_parliamentary_transfer_diagnostic_2026-.decomposition: One bare-stat dice action (Pool=Proposer Influence; Ob=Holder Legitimacy+2) wrapped in a parliamentary vote contest (tally; +1D/-1D/0 bloc modifier) + CB economy (per-pair discrete accumulator). Success/OW => territory TRANSFERS (load-bearing, GD-1 control delta).

verdict.N: PASS — mechanic is necessary (one of three v12c territorial vectors that de-dominate Crown; without it Crown 55-90%).
verdict.R: PARTIAL FAIL — core roll is a bare-stat small-pool dice resolution on a pivotal transfer (sigma-leverage non-uniformity + ~1% wall for weak proposers vs high-L holders) — the exact ED-865/874 pattern. Retry loop (Partial->free CB) is bounded (1/arc throttle + action-slot cost + Failure penalties + last-territory cap) => passes Lesson 5, but Partial 'replaces consumed CB' mildly bypasses the CB-economy damper (design note).
verdict.S: FAIL — resolution paradigm-inconsistent with the migrated faction layer: faction Domain Actions/Suppress/Rebuttal/Treaty now resolve via the d+s resolver; this structurally-identical bare-stat check is still raw dice (authored on the pre-ED-865/874 paradigm; v12c predates the resolver).
verdict.E: PASS — mechanic core legible; 4-mode taxonomy clear.

finding.fix_in_principle: Migrate the core roll to the d+s resolver (M = Proposer Influence - difficulty(Holder Legitimacy)), consistent with the rest of the faction layer.
finding.BLOCKER: BALANCE-LOAD-BEARING. parliamentary_transfer was balance-validated at N=1000 on the DICE engine (v12c: Crown 24.7/Church 28.6/HF 24.2/Varfell 22.5). The resolver's FLOOR 0.05 raises weak-proposer odds off the ~1% wall => more transfers => SHIFTS the validated equilibrium. A naive chat-side method-swap FAILS Stage-4 re-test (introduces a balance regression). The difficulty(L) mapping is a balance parameter that must be SIM-RE-TUNED, not hand-set.
finding.disposition: [OPEN: needs sim re-validation] — NOT applied to canon. The fix is correct in principle but its parameters belong to a simulation re-run of sim/provincial/parliamentary_transfer.py at N=1000, owned by the faction-balance sim line (v12c). Route to that sim session, or Jordan-directed re-tune.
finding.precedent: COIN/structural-contest (matrix entry 2) supports resolver-on-pivotal-transfer in principle; balance equilibrium is the v12c empirical constraint.

_parliamentary_transfer_diagnostic_2026-.design_note: PARL-CB-001: Partial outcome 'replaces consumed CB' lets indefinite re-attempts at no CB cost (action-slot + Failure-risk + 1/arc throttle still apply). Not a Lesson-5 violation; flag for CB-economy intent review.
_parliamentary_transfer_diagnostic_2026-.canon_writes: NONE — candidate only. Unlike ci_political (Treaty rolls not balance-pinned) and peninsular_strain (pure warning extension), this fix requires sim re-validation before any canon change.

_treaty_expiration_diagnostic_2026-05-30.task: resolution-and-balance diagnostic of treaty_expiration_v30 (Stages 1-4) — NEW system, never previously decomposed
_treaty_expiration_diagnostic_2026-05-30.decomposition: (1) Lapse check: per-Treaty independent Bernoulli(0.90) at arc boundary — a TUNED RANDOM EVENT, no actor stat feeds it, memoryless. (2) Re-bind: Senator Outward (Pool=Influence+Standing, Ob=target Sta) — bare-stat, resolver-eligible. (3) Violation: deterministic consequence table.

verdict.N: PASS — the PRIMARY Crown-nerf lever; without it Crown 55-90% (fails balance). Necessary.
verdict.R: PASS — lapse bounded+memoryless, safeguarded by Senator Outward re-bind; re-bind<->lapse is NEGATIVE (self-damping) feedback; violation deterministic; no runaway, no undefined state.
verdict.S: PASS (with note TE-1) — re-bind (Senator Outward) is RAW DICE while sibling faction actions are resolver-governed (ED-865/874). Paradigm-consistency gap, balance-pinned (see TE-1).
verdict.E: PASS — lapse model dead-simple. The 90% 'narrative extremity' the doc flags (§4) is a FEEL critique with a doc-owned deferred alternative (active-maintenance), NOT a NERS resolution defect.

finding.summary: Senator Outward Treaty re-bind is a bare-stat faction action (Influence+Standing vs Ob=target Sta) still on raw dice post-ED-865/874, paradigm-inconsistent with the migrated faction layer.
finding.fix_in_principle: Migrate re-bind to the d+s resolver (M = (Influence+Standing-mod) - difficulty(target Sta)).
finding.BLOCKER: BALANCE-LOAD-BEARING — the re-bind SUCCESS RATE sets the Crown per-arc treaty-maintenance cost the v12c N=1000 equilibrium depends on (Crown 24.7%). Resolver FLOOR/CAP would shift it. Needs SIM re-validation, NOT a chat-side hand-set ratify.
finding.disposition: [OPEN: needs sim re-validation] — identical class & routing as PT-PARL-1. Route to the faction-balance sim line (v12c) / Jordan. NO canon change applied.

_treaty_expiration_diagnostic_2026-05-30.note: 90% lapse narrative-extremity (§4 active-maintenance alternative) is a Jordan FEEL/design decision, doc-owned & deferred (would require sim re-validation). Not a defect; not Claude's to ratify.
_treaty_expiration_diagnostic_2026-05-30.canon_writes: NONE — clean NERS-compliant verdict; the one residual (TE-1) is balance-pinned and sim-deferred.

_faction_succession_split_diagnostic_202.task: resolution-and-balance diagnostic of faction_succession_split_v30 (Stages 1-4) — NEW system, never previously decomposed
_faction_succession_split_diagnostic_202.decomposition: (A) Succession Contest: contenders roll pool vs Ob3, highest net successes wins [dice, mid pools]; (B) margin-based SPLIT trigger (winner margin<2 => faction fragments) [threshold on noisy quantity]; (C) deterministic asset %-split; (D) Disposition gates; (E) Settlement Emergence Order==0 hard threshold. PROVISIONAL (PP-666), NOT v12c-pinned.

verdict_pre.N: PASS — generalized succession is necessary (only Crown succession was modeled; others fell to generic collapse).
verdict_pre.R: FAIL — the SPLIT/UNIFIED structural fork (load-bearing, ~irreversible: re-merge needs both Mandate>=3+treaty) turned on a <2 net-success margin in a small-pool roll: sim showed ~50% split at ALL near-peer successions REGARDLESS of strategic power balance — variance, not strategy, fragmenting the realm.
verdict_pre.S: FAIL — bare highest-net-successes contest + Ob3 counting, paradigm-inconsistent with the migrated resolver faction layer.
verdict_pre.E: FAIL — one roll-margin carried TWO independent roles (WHO wins + WHETHER splits) = Lesson-1 role conflation; player cannot intuit fragmentation.

finding.lens: Lesson 1 (role) + Lesson 3 (small-pool pivotal) + cliff (noisy fork) + Smooth
finding.fix: Two-stage resolution. STAGE 1 (who leads): contested resolver M = strength(top1)-strength(top2). STAGE 2 (whether splits): deterministic strength gap G = strength(top1)-strength(top2): G>=3 UNIFIED, G==2 fractious-unified (Disposition-gated), G<=1 SPLIT; dice tail = leader Overwhelming consolidates a G<=1 split / leader Failure fractures a G>=3. Strength = the EXISTING contest pool (no new variable). Ob3/net-success counting retired for the fork.
finding.precedent: Succession-war fragmentation tracks claimant power balance (Carolingian divisio, Diadochi, Ottoman) — near-peer partition, dominant consolidate. COIN/structural-contest resolver shape (matrix entry 2).
finding.stage4: N pass (no new variable), R pass (fragmentation tracks strategic gap, no variance fork), S pass (resolver-consistent, gap-band not noisy cliff), E pass+improved (WHO and WHETHER-SPLIT separated -> role-conflation resolved; legible). Loops unchanged-bounded.
finding.disposition: RATIFIED to canon (doc was PROVISIONAL, NOT v12c-pinned => no balance equilibrium to protect; unlike PT-PARL-1/TE-1). Precedent-anchored under design-authority grant.

applied_canon: designs/provincial/faction_succession_split_v30.md §2.2 (two-stage resolution), §2.3 (gap-band split fork + rationale + dice tail), §3 (worked example recomputed)

_faction_behavior_diagnostic_2026-05-30.task: resolution-and-balance diagnostic of faction_behavior_v30 (PP-686 v2, Class-A) — NEW system, never previously decomposed
_faction_behavior_diagnostic_2026-05-30.decomposition: Class-A behavior architecture (Mission/Cascade/Public-Expectation/Legitimacy+Popular-Support). Mostly DETERMINISTIC accounting (no dice). The one resolution-relevant output is §3.7 Domain Action Ob_modifier feeding the (resolver-governed) DA roll. Stage-10 sim PASS (12/14 battery); ±3->±2 dominant-strategy fix already applied (C1).

verdict.N: PASS — replaces legacy Ethical Framework Modifiers; Legitimacy/Popular-Support split is necessary (Mandate-as-single-scalar lost information).
verdict.R: PASS — §3.7 Ob_modifier clamped ±2; all feedback loops bounded+damped (hard caps: Legitimacy/PS [0,7], cascade_fidelity [-1,1], Ob ±2, λ<1, drift_coef 0.6). Crisis-bypass (scars>=3 suspends damping) is undamped-but-BOUNDED (intended visible-instability window; downstream still clamped) => PASSES Lesson 5 (not undamped+unbounded).
verdict.S: PASS — §3.7 Ob_modifier feeds the d+s resolver and INHERITS its uniform Ob->probability impact (a +1 Ob = constant -0.20 P by ED-865/874 design); Lesson-2 satisfied downstream; consistent with the migrated faction layer.
verdict.E: PASS (with caveat) — architecture is complex (4 components, cascade graphs, 13-Conviction vectors) but the complexity is load-bearing (dynamic emergent behavior) and the player-facing OUTPUT is a legible ±2 Ob shift. Necessity-justified.

notes: The headline dominant-strategy risk (±3 producing dominant-strategy at high alignment) was ALREADY caught and fixed (C1 ±2 cap) and sim-validated (v1+v2). No re-finding.

§3.7 rounding (strictness crossing 0.5 flips expectation term 0->±1) is a gentle threshold on a SLOW accumulator (Legitimacy/PS integrate over seasons), not an accidental cliff on a fast input — acceptable/legible.
Mandate now DERIVED (0.5L+0.5PS) — Lesson-1 role-split already done correctly (L=acceptance, PS=active backing carry distinct jobs).
